title: Sausage rolls
subtitle: Seasoned sausagemeat in flaky all-butter pastry
excerpt: Good-quality sausagemeat seasoned with mustard and herbs, wrapped in all-butter puff pastry and baked until the pastry is shatteringly flaky and the filling is cooked through. Better than any bakery version when made with proper sausagemeat and real butter pastry. Good hot or cold.
sourceNotes: The sausage roll is a British staple at picnics, parties, and bakery counters. The modern form uses ready-made puff pastry, which has made sausage rolls a fast bake. The filling is improved by seasoning the raw sausagemeat with mustard, fennel seeds, or sage before wrapping.

content.text: Use all-butter puff pastry. The difference in flavour and texture between all-butter and vegetable fat pastry is significant when the pastry is the main ingredient. Remove the sausagemeat from its casings if using whole sausages rather than bought sausagemeat.

Heat the oven to 200°C. Mix the {{sausagemeat}} sausagemeat with the {{dijon-mustard}}, {{fennel-seeds}} if using, {{sea-salt-fine}}, and {{black-pepper}}. Roll out the {{puff-pastry}} to a rectangle approximately 30 x 40 cm. Cut in half lengthways to make two long strips. Form the sausagemeat into a log along the length of each pastry strip, placing it along one edge. Brush the opposite edge with beaten {{eggs}}. Roll the pastry over the sausagemeat and press the edges to seal. Cut each roll into 8 pieces. Score the top of each with a knife. Brush all over with egg wash. Bake on a lined baking tray for 22 to 25 minutes until deep golden and the filling is cooked through.

items.fix: Preheat the oven fully before the sausage rolls go in. Egg wash is essential for colour and shine
items.cause: Oven not fully preheated, or egg wash not applied
items.symptom: Pastry pale and soft rather than golden and flaky

items.fix: This is expected. It does not affect the flavour. Filling the pastry more generously reduces the visible gap
items.cause: Normal, sausagemeat contracts as it cooks
items.symptom: Filling shrinks away from the pastry during baking

items.fix: Use a dark metal baking tray or line a tray with foil. Perforated baking trays allow heat to reach the bottom of the pastry directly
items.cause: Baking on a cold or non-perforated tray

content.text: Sausage rolls appear at every British party, picnic, and buffet table. They are equally good hot from the oven and cold several hours later. The homemade version scales easily for crowds and freezes well unbaked.
